commands.review_summary:
The PR centralizes a privacy-minimal success fallback for confirmed messaging sends when tool-result middleware fails, and adds Codex, embedded-runner, and middleware regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +65, Tests +203. Total +268 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. From source inspection, current main executes `message.send` before tool-result middleware and still replaces thrown or invalid post-middleware output with the generic middleware-error result.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review remains appropriate because the remaining blocker is proof and merge judgment, not a narrow code repair for an automated worker.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve delivered message send results
target.body:
## Summary

- snapshot confirmed provider delivery before result sanitization or middleware can mutate the raw result
- keep generic middleware fail-closed behavior, but return a minimal privacy-safe success acknowledgment when a successful messaging send already has a provider message receipt
- implement the invariant once in the middleware failure owner so embedded and Codex runtimes behave identically without a new plugin SDK export

This replaces the earlier result-shape parser with the repository's canonical messaging-delivery classifier and does not expose raw provider payloads after thrown, invalid, or explicit middleware failure.

Fixes #84271

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a successful Slack-shaped `message(action=send)` result with a provider `messageId` remains successful when result middleware mutates the raw object and then throws, returns an invalid result, or returns an explicit middleware failure; ambiguous or deferred internal sends remain failed.

Real environment tested: OpenClaw embedded and Codex runtime bridges on Node 22+, plus Linux AWS Crabbox changed gates and production build.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/agents/harness/tool-result-middleware.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-message-tool-source-reply.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.extensions.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: 112 focused tests passed. Codex bridge coverage executes the tool, snapshots the successful nested Slack receipt, allows middleware to remove the receipt and throw, and verifies the returned dynamic-tool response remains `success: true` without exposing the raw result. Additional tests cover oversized details collapsing after receipt capture, explicit middleware-error results, and forged receipt insertion on a deferred internal source reply.

Observed result after fix: confirmed sends return `Message delivered, but result post-processing failed.` with `ok: true`; middleware failures without pre-middleware provider receipt evidence retain `Tool output unavailable due to post-processing error.` and `success: false`.

What was not tested: no additional live Slack message was posted; the regression path uses the production runtime bridges with the real Slack result envelope shape and synthetic identifiers.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/harness/tool-result-middleware.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-message-tool-source-reply.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.extensions.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts` — 112 passed
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check && pnpm plugin-sdk:check-exports` — passed
- fresh autoreview — clean after addressing receipt provenance, sanitization ordering, internal-source, explicit-error, and SDK-surface findings

## Dependency contract

Codex app-server consumes the client `success` boolean as the dynamic-tool function output success value, so the bridge must report a confirmed prior side effect as successful even when later result processing fails. Verified in sibling Codex source: `codex-rs/app-server/src/dynamic_tools.rs`, `codex-rs/core/src/tools/handlers/dynamic.rs`, and `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2/item.rs`.
target.author: zerone0x
